pattern: A run of the word only needs a fresh group of 'abc' the moment a character fails to continue strictly past the previous one, so counting those restarts and comparing against three times the group count gives the missing character total directly.
hints: Imagine splitting the word into as few runs as possible where each run is a strictly increasing subsequence of the letters a, b, c.
A new run has to start exactly when a character does not come strictly after the one before it.
Each run will eventually be padded out to a full three-letter group, so the answer is three times the number of runs minus the word's own length.

edge_inputs.description: already a perfectly valid single group
letters skip forward within one group
single repeated character forces one new group per character
backward jump forces a new group
two clean groups back to back
mixture of valid runs and repeats

stress.description: random string over a, b, c
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(50, size % 50 + 1))
    word = ''.join(rng.choice('abc') for _ in range(n))
    return [word]
